name: sprites
description: Manage Sprites.dev stateful AI sandboxes — list, create, inspect, and destroy persistent Linux VMs and execute commands inside them

envVars.name: SPRITES_TOKEN
envVars.description: Sprites API token in org/token-id/secret format, generated at sprites.dev/account; same env var used by the Sprites CLI and SDKs

tools.name: List Sprites
tools.description: List sprites in the organization, optionally filtered by name prefix and paginated

continuation_token.description: Token from a previous response for pagination

tools.name: Create Sprite
tools.description: Create a new sprite — a persistent, hardware-isolated Linux VM that keeps its filesystem state between runs

name.description: Unique name for the sprite within the organization

url_settings.description: URL access configuration, e.g. {"auth":"sprite"} (default) or {"auth":"public"} to make the sprite URL publicly reachable

tools.name: Get Sprite
tools.description: Get a sprite by name, including its URL, runtime status (cold, warm, or running), and creation/activity timestamps

tools.name: Destroy Sprite
tools.description: Destroy a sprite by name, permanently deleting the VM and its persisted filesystem state

name.description: Unique name of the sprite to destroy

tools.name: Execute Command
tools.description: Execute a command inside a sprite over HTTP (non-interactive) and return its output as JSON

name.description: Unique name of the sprite to run the command in

cmd.description: Command and its arguments, one array entry per argument (sent as repeated cmd query parameters), e.g. ["ls", "-la"]

env.description: Environment variables in KEY=VALUE format, one array entry per variable

tools.name: Create Checkpoint
tools.description: Create a point-in-time checkpoint of a sprite (filesystem and memory) before risky operations; the response is a stream of NDJSON progress events

name.description: Unique name of the sprite to checkpoint

tools.name: List Checkpoints
tools.description: List a sprite's checkpoints with their IDs (e.g. v7), creation times, and comments — discover restore points

tools.name: Restore Checkpoint
tools.description: Roll a sprite back to a checkpoint, replacing its current state with the snapshot; the response is a stream of NDJSON progress events

name.description: Unique name of the sprite to restore

checkpointId.description: Checkpoint ID from List Checkpoints, e.g. v7

prompts.title: My sprites
prompts.prompt: List my Sprites.dev sandboxes with their status and last activity.

prompts.title: Spin up a sprite
prompts.prompt: Create a new sprite named scratchpad and confirm it is reachable by running uname -a inside it.

prompts.title: Clean up sprites
prompts.prompt: List my sprites and destroy any that I confirm are no longer needed.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Sprites setup

steps.title: Create a Sprites account
steps.description: Sign up at https://sprites.dev by signing in with a Fly.io account (Sprites is made by Fly.io); new accounts can create sprites right away for testing.

steps.title: Generate an API token
steps.description: Go to https://sprites.dev/account and generate an API token. Tokens have the format my-org/token-id/secret. (Alternatively, install the sprite CLI and run sprite org auth.)

steps.title: Set the environment variable
steps.description: Copy the token into your .env as SPRITES_TOKEN.

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run List Sprites to confirm the token works, then create a sprite and execute a command in it.

notes: The API is served on https://api.sprites.dev/v1 and authenticates with Authorization: Bearer $SPRITES_TOKEN
Sprites are stateful: the filesystem persists across suspends, and sprites wake automatically on use (status moves between cold, warm, and running)
Destroying a sprite permanently deletes its persisted state

Execute Command uses the HTTP POST exec endpoint for non-interactive commands; interactive TTY sessions, filesystem APIs, checkpoints, and services are WebSocket/SDK-first and not covered by these tools
